---
title: "VLA Fundamentals"
---

Vision-language-action models combine visual understanding, instruction grounding, and action generation in one policy system. This guide summarizes core VLA concepts and maps them to the repository roadmap.

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Practical meaning in this toolchain |
| --- | --- |
| Vision encoder | Converts camera observations into latent features for control |
| Language conditioning | Encodes task intent or instruction context |
| Action head | Produces joint or control actions from fused multimodal context |
| Embodiment transfer | Adapting policy behavior across robot hardware variants |
| Fine-tuning strategy | LoRA or full-parameter updates based on compute and latency targets |

## Why VLA Matters

| Driver | Impact |
| --- | --- |
| Multi-task scaling | One policy family can cover broader skill sets |
| Natural instruction interfaces | Language prompts can parameterize behavior goals |
| Foundation model reuse | Pretrained visual/language priors reduce task-specific data burden |

## Architecture Tradeoffs

| Axis | Option A | Option B | Decision impact |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Action representation | Continuous | Tokenized/discrete | Latency, smoothness, and deployment complexity |
| Adaptation | LoRA | Full fine-tuning | Cost, storage, and iteration speed |
| Inference strategy | Synchronous | Asynchronous pipeline | Throughput and control frequency |

## Integration Considerations

| Area | Guidance |
| --- | --- |
| Data interface | Align VLA training data with LeRobot-compatible schema where possible |
| Runtime constraints | Validate control frequency and end-to-end latency for SIL and edge paths |
| Evaluation | Use SIL scenarios before robot runtime promotion |
| Registry strategy | Track base model, adapter version, and dataset lineage in model metadata |

## Current Repository Status

VLA implementation is currently specification-first and roadmap-driven. Use the specification document for detailed model options, staged implementation, and integration architecture.

## Next Steps

- [Vision-Language-Action Models: Integration Specification](https://github.com/microsoft/physical-ai-toolchain/blob/main/docs/vla-specification.md)
- [IL to RL Warm-Start Strategies](https://github.com/microsoft/physical-ai-toolchain/blob/main/docs/il-to-rl-warm-start-strategies.md)
- [Training Guide](../training/overview.qmd)
